name: JW Marriott Indianapolis
skywalkNote: Direct climate-controlled. Gold standard. Shortest to exhibit hall.
skywalkZones: Exhibit Hall, RPG rooms, Circle Centre via IndyPlace network
connectingNote: Large property — best connecting inventory in block. Request at booking.
pointsRedemptionNote: Must book outside block. ~60-80K pts/night. Loses block rate guarantee.
elevatorNote: Key-required access reduces crowding. Some find key requirement annoying for guests visiting your room.
noiseNote: Convention energy hub. Loud common areas Thu-Sat. Rooms are well-insulated.
staffNote: Consistently highest-rated. Staff engages with cosplay and game talk. Conde Nast recognized.
checkinNote: Wednesday 2-6pm is brutal. Use Marriott Bonvoy app for mobile check-in to skip the line entirely.
lateCheckoutNote: Available on request. Marriott Bonvoy Platinum+ gets guaranteed late checkout.
housekeepingNote: Daily on request. Towel refresh automatic.
outletNote: Standard 4-6 outlets per room. Suites have more. Bring a power strip.
lobbyGamingNote: Top-tier hub. 2nd/3rd floor dedicated game tables. Staff encourages it. THE social hotel of Gen Con.
packageNote: Accepts packages. Ship to: Guest Name, JW Marriott, 10 S West St, Indianapolis IN 46204. Arriving for Gen Con [dates].
rideshare: Maryland St side entrance, not main lobby. Can be confusing — tell driver 'Maryland Street entrance.'
pharmacy: CVS 336 S Delaware St, 8 min walk
grocery: Circle Centre CVS 5 min (basics). Kroger 1217 S East St 1.5 mi (full).
pros: Best skywalk. Convention social hub. Multiple dining. Huge property. Best connecting room inventory.
cons: Highest rates. $59-80 parking. Elevator key required. Loud convention energy.
veteranTip: If your lottery slot is before 12:25pm, book this. It's the consensus #1 for a reason. Request a lower floor (8-15) for faster elevator access.

name: Hyatt Regency Indianapolis
skywalkNote: Attached to ICC itself. Also connects to Lucas Oil and Artsgarden.
skywalkZones: Exhibit Hall, RPG rooms, Lucas Oil, Artsgarden — all direct
parkNearby: Third-party self-park nearby ~$40/day. Cannot charge to room.
pointsRedemptionNote: Must book outside block. ~25-40K pts/night. Hyatt points are high-value.
elevatorNote: Insufficient count for building height. Guests on floor 7+ report 5-10 min waits during peak. Request lower floors.
noiseNote: Attached to ICC means convention foot traffic through lobby all day. Rooms insulated but hallways busy.
staffNote: Professional and accommodating. Not as enthusiastic as JW but no issues reported.
checkinNote: Wednesday lines long. World of Hyatt app check-in available.
lateCheckoutNote: Globalist members get guaranteed 4pm late checkout.
outletNote: Standard outlets. Bring a strip for group sharing.
lobbyGamingNote: Second-tier lobby gaming. Happens organically but not cultivated like JW. Lobby seating is comfortable.
packageNote: $5/package fee. Ship to hotel attention guest name.
rideshare: Capitol Ave entrance. Easier pickup than JW.
pharmacy: CVS 336 S Delaware St, 7 min walk
grocery: Circle Centre CVS 5 min. Kroger 1.5 mi.
pros: Attached to ICC. Best Lucas Oil access. Indoor pool. Multiple dining. Veteran's #1 for pure access.
cons: Elevator waits notorious. Valet-only self-park. Loud common areas. Convention foot traffic through lobby.
veteranTip: Request floor 3-6 to avoid elevator hell. The Artsgarden connection is underrated — quiet route to south ICC.

name: Westin Indianapolis
skywalkNote: Direct shot to Exhibitor Hall via Circle Centre. Fewer twists than JW route.
skywalkZones: Exhibit Hall via Circle Centre, IndyPlace network
noiseNote: Quieter than JW or Hyatt. Rooms well-insulated. Heavenly Beds help.
staffNote: Friendly, professional. Good Gen Con rapport.
outletNote: Standard. Heavenly Bed rooms have bedside USB.
lobbyGamingNote: Known lobby gaming spot. Heavenly Beds crowd is social. Good tables.
pharmacy: CVS 336 S Delaware St, 7 min walk
grocery: Circle Centre CVS 4 min. Kroger 1.5 mi.
pros: Indoor pool+hot tub. Great skywalk route. Fridge in room. Lower parking. Heavenly Beds. Lobby gaming.
cons: Parking NO in-and-out. Slightly older feel. No microwave.
veteranTip: Community sleeper pick. Best balance of price, access, and amenities. The pool+hot tub after a long con day is therapeutic.

name: Embassy Suites Downtown
skywalkNote: Via Circle Centre Mall. Indoor the whole way.
skywalkZones: Circle Centre, then ICC via mall connection
connectingNote: Suite layout reduces need — parlor+bedroom sleeps 4-6.
breakfastNote: FREE made-to-order breakfast buffet + FREE evening reception (alcohol + snacks 5:30-7:30pm daily).
pointsRedemptionNote: Outside block. ~60-80K pts/night. Hilton 5th-night-free on points makes 5-night stays cheaper.
elevatorNote: LEGENDARY wait times. 10+ min during peak. Atrium creates single-point congestion. Veterans request floor 3-4 specifically to use stairs.
noiseNote: Atrium design means noise carries across all floors. Rooms facing atrium are noisier AND brighter (morning light). Request outer-facing room.
staffNote: Friendly but overwhelmed during peak con hours. Patient.
checkinNote: Known for long Wednesday lines. Hilton Honors app check-in helps.
outletNote: Suites have more outlets than standard rooms. Living area + bedroom = 6-8 outlets.
lobbyGamingNote: Atrium is the MOST gaming-friendly space. Plentiful tables. Staff allows it. The happy hour crowd naturally games.
pharmacy: CVS 336 S Delaware St, 8 min walk
grocery: Circle Centre CVS 4 min. Kroger 1.5 mi.
pros: FREE breakfast+evening drinks save $$$. All suites (living area = game room). Fridge+microwave. Indoor pool. Atrium gaming hub. Sofa bed sleeps 6.
cons: NOTORIOUS elevators. Atrium noise. Check-in lines. Can feel chaotic.
veteranTip: The #1 value pick. Breakfast saves $15-25/person/day. Evening reception replaces dinner. Suite parlor IS your game room. Request floor 3-4, outer-facing, and use stairs.

name: Conrad Indianapolis
skywalkNote: Via Circle Centre Mall — very long indoor walk. 2-block street walk is faster.
skywalkZones: Circle Centre only — ICC requires exiting mall
pool: Rooftop infinity pool (seasonal) + hot tub + sauna + steam
elevatorNote: Small property means fewer guests competing. Generally fine.
noiseNote: Quietest luxury option. Small property, upscale crowd. The 'escape the con' hotel.
staffNote: Professional luxury service. Not gamer-enthusiastic but respectful.
cosplayNote: Upscale environment. Elaborate cosplay gets looks but no reported issues.
lateCheckoutNote: Usually available on request. Diamond guaranteed.
housekeepingNote: Full daily housekeeping standard at luxury tier.
outletNote: Luxury rooms have more outlets + USB. Nespresso machine uses one.
lobbyGamingNote: Not a gaming lobby. Quiet, upscale. This is where you go to NOT be at the convention.
pharmacy: CVS 336 S Delaware St, 6 min walk
grocery: Circle Centre CVS 3 min. Kroger 1.5 mi.
pros: Most luxurious. Rooftop pool+sauna. Capital Grille. Quiet retreat. Fridge. Nespresso. Art collection. Fast check-in.
cons: Most expensive. Skywalk is a lie (walk outside). Only 247 rooms — sells out FIRST. $200 incidental hold. Not gamer-culture.
veteranTip: Sells out fastest in the lottery. The introvert's dream hotel. Walk outside to ICC — the indoor route through Circle Centre takes longer.

name: Fairfield Inn Downtown
skywalkNote: Part of IndyPlace skywalk network. Direct.
pointsRedemptionNote: Outside block. ~35-50K pts/night. Best Bonvoy value in block.
elevatorNote: Small property, low floors. Generally fine.
noiseNote: Small, no frills, low foot traffic.
outletNote: Budget rooms have fewer outlets. Bring a power strip.
lobbyGamingNote: Small lobby. Gaming happens in IndyPlace common areas instead.
pros: CHEAPEST downtown with skywalk. Free breakfast. Fridge+microwave. Best budget pick.
cons: Basic rooms. Small. No on-site bar. Limited dining.
veteranTip: Maximum value downtown. Free breakfast + fridge + microwave = lowest total food cost of any downtown hotel. The room is just a place to sleep — you'll be at the con all day anyway.

name: SpringHill Suites Downtown
connectingNote: Suite layout with separate areas reduces need.
outletNote: Suite layout has more outlets than standard rooms.
lobbyGamingNote: Suite living areas make great private game rooms for groups.
pros: Suite layout = in-room game table. Free breakfast. Fridge+microwave. Skywalk. Sofa bed sleeps extra. Groups love it.
veteranTip: The group pick. Suite living area IS your private game room. 4 people in a studio suite with sofa bed is the most fun-per-dollar downtown.

name: Indianapolis Marriott Downtown
elevatorNote: Large property but adequate elevator count.
outletNote: Recently renovated rooms have better outlet placement.
lobbyGamingNote: Cool lobby tables. Some gaming but not a primary hub.
pros: Recently renovated. Skywalk. Large property. Good connecting rooms.
cons: 5-NIGHT MINIMUM (Tue-Sat). No pool. No fridge. Extra night adds ~$270+ to total.
veteranTip: Do the math: 5 nights at $270 = $1,350 vs 4 nights at $295 at JW = $1,180. The 5th night penalty is real. Only book if you want Tuesday arrival.

name: Sheraton City Centre
noiseNote: Consistently recommended as quietest downtown option. Off the main corridor.
staffNote: Excellent staff reputation specifically for Gen Con crowd.
lobbyGamingNote: Not a primary hub. Bowling alley is the social draw.
pros: QUIETEST downtown. Rooftop pool with Monument Circle views. Bowling alley on-site. Great staff. Solid value.
cons: Outdoor pool only (fine in July). More business feel. Slightly north of main corridor. No fridge.
veteranTip: The sleep-quality pick. If you prioritize rest over being in the thick of it, Sheraton + bowling + rooftop pool is an underrated combo.

name: Crowne Plaza Union Station
skywalkZones: Direct skybridge to ICC south end near Lucas Oil
pointsRedemptionNote: IHG uses FIXED point pricing regardless of demand date. 30-35K pts/night = best points value in block. Book outside block for points.
noiseNote: Historic building character. Some rooms have uneven sound insulation due to conversion.
outletNote: Historic conversion means some rooms have limited outlets. Bring strips.
lobbyGamingNote: Grand hall is atmospheric but not a gaming hub.
pros: Historic Union Station. Direct skybridge. Best Lucas Oil access. Fridge. IHG points = best value. Pullman train car rooms are iconic.
cons: No pool. Uneven room quality (historic conversion). Limited valet. No connecting rooms.
veteranTip: The points play. IHG fixed pricing makes this the best loyalty redemption in the block. The Pullman sleeper car rooms are a once-in-a-lifetime novelty — book one if you can.

name: Hampton Inn Downtown
skywalkNote: No skywalk. 2 blocks to ICC, short outdoor walk.
lobbyGamingNote: Small lobby. Not a gaming space.
pros: Free hot breakfast. Fridge+microwave. Only 2 blocks to ICC. Budget-friendly. Closest to CVS pharmacy.
cons: No pool. No bar. No restaurant. No skywalk.
veteranTip: Pure budget utility. Free breakfast + fridge + microwave + 2 blocks to ICC. You're paying for a bed and saving money on everything else.

name: Omni Severin Hotel
skywalkNote: Skywalk via Circle Centre may be permanently closed (ICC construction). 1-block street walk is standard.
skywalkZones: Uncertain — verify for 2026. Street walk is reliable.
pointsRedemptionNote: Omni's program is smaller. Points value lower than Marriott/Hilton.
noiseNote: Near Georgia St — some street noise on south-facing rooms.
staffNote: Recently renovated. Good reputation with Gen Con crowd. Historic charm.
lobbyGamingNote: Some gaming but not primary hub. Historic bar is the social draw.
pros: Indoor pool. 2 restaurants. 1 block to ICC. Historic 1913 charm. Fridge. Recently renovated. Beloved by community.
cons: Valet only. Skywalk may be closed. No mobile check-in. Older building.
veteranTip: The community favorite that flies under the radar. Recently renovated rooms are excellent. The skywalk status is the big question mark for 2026 — verify before committing.

name: Hilton Indianapolis Hotel & Suites
breakfastNote: No. 120 West Bar & Grill + McCormick & Schmick's.
noiseNote: Far enough from main corridor to be quieter. Market St has some traffic.
lobbyGamingNote: Topgolf Swing Suite is the social draw. Not a traditional gaming lobby.
pros: TWO pools (unique). Topgolf Swing Suite. 2 restaurants. Good mid-range price. Fridge+microwave. Suites sleep 6. Connecting rooms.
cons: No skywalk. 8 min outdoor walk. Farther from action.
veteranTip: The Topgolf suite is genuinely fun as a group activity. Two pools means you can always find one uncrowded. Best for groups who don't mind the walk.

name: The Alexander
skywalkNote: No skywalk. 10-12 min outdoor walk. CityWay neighborhood.
noiseNote: CityWay is residential. Quietest neighborhood option.
staffNote: Boutique service. Professional but not gamer-focused.
cosplayNote: Art hotel environment. Elaborate cosplay may draw attention.
lobbyGamingNote: Not a gaming space. Art-forward quiet retreat.
rideshare: Delaware St. CityWay is easier for rideshare than downtown core — less congestion.
grocery: CityWay has some options. Kroger 1.2 mi.
quickFood: Less walkable to main con food
pros: Boutique art hotel. Great restaurant. Quiet CityWay retreat. Fridge. Best rideshare pickup logistics (less congestion).
cons: 10-12 min walk, no skywalk. No pool. Least convenient for frequent con trips. Far from everything.
veteranTip: The introvert's pick and the rideshare pick. If you're Ubering anyway (suburban hotel canceled, etc.), Alexander + easy rideshare is actually competitive. Beautiful hotel, just far.

name: Le Meridien Indianapolis
skywalkNote: Connects to ICC and Circle Centre. Very close.
coffee: Le Meridien signature coffee ritual in lobby
noiseNote: Boutique quiet. Small property = less foot traffic.
staffNote: Art-forward service. Unique Le Meridien culture.
lobbyGamingNote: Art-forward boutique. Not a gaming lobby.
pros: Closest to ICC (tied with Hyatt). Boutique art-forward. Skywalk. Great restaurant. Fridge. Quiet.
cons: No pool. Only 157 rooms — sells out very fast. Pricey. No connecting rooms.
veteranTip: Tiny and premium. Second-fastest sellout after Conrad. If quiet + proximity is your priority and you have an early lottery slot, grab this.

name: Courtyard Capitol
skywalkNote: Connected via IndyPlace network but longest route. Behind State Capitol.
pointsRedemptionNote: Outside block. ~30-45K pts. Good Bonvoy value.
staffNote: Standard service. Free bikes are a nice touch.
lobbyGamingNote: Free bikes to explore. Not a gaming lobby.
quickFood: Downtown core 8 min walk — less walkable
pros: Affordable IndyPlace with skywalk. Indoor+outdoor pool. Free bikes. Fridge+microwave. Connecting rooms. Quiet.
cons: Farthest IndyPlace property. Long skywalk route. Behind Capitol. Less walkable to food.
veteranTip: Free bikes are actually useful for quick trips to food. If you can handle the extra walk time, this is the quietest + cheapest IndyPlace option with a pool.

name: Hilton Garden Inn Downtown
skywalkNote: Connected via indoor walkways but longest practical route (0.6 mi). Many walk outside instead.
skywalkZones: Technically connected — most people walk outside
noiseNote: North of main corridor. Less convention foot traffic. Quieter option.
staffNote: TripAdvisor review: 'Best hotel for Gencon!' Staff friendly.
outletNote: Garden Inn standard includes better outlet placement. In-room Keurig.
lobbyGamingNote: Quieter lobby. Some gaming but not a hub.
pros: Indoor pool+whirlpool. Fridge+microwave. On-site restaurant. Keurig. Convenience store. Connecting rooms. Quiet.
cons: 0.6 mi real walk. Longest practical skywalk. Far from food truck corridor.
veteranTip: The 'I want quiet and amenities and don't mind walking' pick. In-room Keurig + on-site convenience store + fridge+microwave = self-sufficient base camp.
